meta.name: Korean Fried Chicken (Yangnyeom & Soy-Garlic Options)
meta.subtitle: Extra-crispy double-fried chicken with classic Korean sticky glaze options.

source.name: ssa.catalogs.cuisines.korean
source.notes: Canonical SSA KFC: double-fry method, batter/starch crust, and two primary sauces (yangnyeom + soy-garlic). Includes oven/air-fryer adaptations and safety checks.

yield.notes: Makes ~900–1100 g cooked chicken depending on cut; best eaten hot and crispy.

notes: shatter-crisp crust
sticky glaze (optional)
garlic-forward (soy-garlic)
gochujang chili-sweet (yangnyeom)

required.label: Instant-read thermometer
required.notes: For oil temp and chicken doneness.

required.label: Wire rack
required.notes: For draining; keeps crust crisp (avoid paper towels).

optional.label: Air fryer
optional.notes: Use the baked/air-fried variation (not as shatter-crisp as double-fry).

optional.label: Oven
optional.notes: Use baked variation if deep frying is not possible.

notes: Use gluten-free soy sauce/tamari and ensure gochujang is gluten-free if needed.
Some recipes use wheat flour; this version uses potato/corn starch as primary for crispness.

ingredients.label: Chicken wings (drumettes & flats), split if needed
ingredients.notes: You can also use boneless thigh chunks (see variations).

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: About 1 3/4 tsp kosher; adjust to your salt.

ingredients.label: Soju / vodka / rice wine (for de-odorizing)
ingredients.notes: Optional; helps reduce poultry odor. Do not overdo.

ingredients.label: Rice flour
ingredients.notes: Optional for extra crisp; otherwise replace with more starch.

ingredients.label: Baking powder
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 tsp; improves airy, craggy crust.

ingredients.label: Egg
ingredients.notes: Optional—some KFC styles use only starch. Egg helps coating adhesion.

ingredients.label: Neutral frying oil (canola/peanut/soybean)
ingredients.notes: Use enough oil for 5–7 cm depth in your pot; amount varies by pot size.

ingredients.label: Sauce choice: pick Yangnyeom OR Soy-Garlic (ingredients below)
ingredients.notes: You can also serve plain with a light sprinkle of salt and sesame.

ingredients.label: Gochugaru (Yangnyeom, optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional: deeper chili flavor; omit for milder sauce.

ingredients.label: Rice vinegar or lemon juice (Soy-Garlic glaze)

notes: Hot oil can cause severe burns. Use a deep pot, keep kids away, and avoid overfilling.
Maintain oil temperature for crispness and safety; avoid adding wet chicken.
Cook chicken to safe internal temperature (74°C / 165°F).
If oil smokes, it is too hot—remove from heat briefly.
Fry in batches to prevent boil-over and temperature drop.

fridge.notes: Sauced chicken softens faster; store sauce separately when possible.

notes: Re-crisp unsauced chicken in oven/air fryer at 200°C / 400°F for 8–12 minutes.
If already sauced, reheat gently and accept softer crust.

adaptations.label: Air fryer (lower mess, less crisp than double-fry)

notes: Use the same seasoning and coating, but spray coated chicken lightly with oil.
Air fry at 200°C / 390°F for 18–22 minutes, flipping halfway, until 74°C / 165°F internal.
Sauce after cooking; optionally return to air fryer 2 minutes to set glaze.
Bake on a wire rack over a sheet pan at 220°C / 425°F for 35–45 minutes, flipping once.
Spray with oil for browning. Sauce after baking.
Use 900 g boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 3–4 cm chunks.
Fry slightly shorter: first fry 5–6 minutes, second fry 2–3 minutes, until 74°C / 165°F.
Choose soy-garlic glaze OR reduce gochujang to 1–2 tbsp and omit gochugaru in yangnyeom.

variations.label: Half-and-half (two sauces)
notes: Split fried chicken into two bowls. Toss half in yangnyeom and half in soy-garlic for variety.

variations.label: Plain crispy (no sauce)
notes: Serve unsauced with a light sprinkle of salt and sesame; add dipping sauce on the side.
The signature crisp comes from a starch-heavy coating + double-frying.
Keep fried chicken on a rack (not paper towels) to preserve crunch.
Sauce just before serving; saucing early will soften the crust.

steps.title: Prep chicken and dry it well
instructions: Pat chicken very dry with paper towels (dry surface = crisp crust, safer frying).
Season with salt, pepper, and optional garlic/ginger powder.
Optional: add 1 tbsp soju/vodka/rice wine; toss briefly.

steps.title: Mix coating
instructions: In a bowl, whisk potato starch (or cornstarch), optional rice flour, and baking powder until uniform.
Optional egg method: in a separate bowl, beat 1 egg. (If using, dip chicken in egg before coating.)
checks: Coating is evenly mixed; baking powder is distributed.

steps.title: Coat chicken
instructions: Working in batches, coat chicken thoroughly in starch mix, pressing lightly to adhere.
Shake off excess and place on a rack for 5–10 minutes to hydrate slightly (helps crust set).
checks: Chicken looks evenly dusted; no large dry clumps falling off.

steps.title: Heat oil and first fry (cook-through)
instructions: Heat oil to 165–170°C / 330–340°F in a deep pot.
Fry chicken in small batches 7–8 minutes (wings) until pale golden and cooked through.
Transfer to rack. Let rest 10–12 minutes while you fry remaining batches.
checks: Oil temp stays in range (avoid crowding).
Chicken reaches 74°C / 165°F at thickest point (or will after second fry).

steps.title: Make sauce (choose one)
instructions: While chicken rests, make ONE sauce:
Yangnyeom: In a small saucepan over low heat, combine ketchup, gochujang, honey/rice syrup, soy sauce, rice vinegar, optional gochugaru, and minced garlic. Simmer 2–3 minutes until glossy.
Soy-Garlic: In a small saucepan over low heat, combine soy sauce, brown sugar, honey, minced garlic, minced ginger, and rice vinegar/lemon. Simmer 2–3 minutes; finish with sesame oil off heat.
checks: Sauce is glossy and lightly thickened; not burnt or overly reduced.

steps.title: Second fry (crisp and deepen color)
instructions: Increase oil temp to 175–180°C / 350–355°F.
Refry chicken in batches for 2–3 minutes until deep golden and very crisp.
Drain on rack for 2 minutes.
checks: Crust is audibly crisp; chicken is deeply golden without burning.

steps.title: Toss in sauce and finish
instructions: For sauced chicken: toss hot fried chicken with warm sauce in a large bowl until evenly coated.
For extra crisp: sauce lightly or serve sauce on the side.
Finish with toasted sesame seeds and sliced scallions if using.
Serve immediately with pickled radish or slaw.
checks: Chicken is served quickly after saucing to preserve crunch.
